questions.explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal mechanical waves. Particles vibrate parallel to the direction of wave propagation.
Sound requires a material medium (solid, liquid, or gas) to propagate. It cannot travel through vacuum.

questions.text: What is the audible range of human hearing?
questions.explanation: Humans can hear sounds with frequencies between 20 Hz and 20,000 Hz. Below 20 Hz is infrasound, above 20 kHz is ultrasound.

questions.text: The speed of sound is maximum in ________.
questions.explanation: Speed of sound is maximum in solids, less in liquids, and minimum in gases. This is because particles are closest in solids.
